description_long: This is a single-story bungalow with a sprawling, elongated footprint and a cross-shaped roof layout. The roof is a combination of hip and gable forms, covered in gray shingles, and features a prominent central section with lower wings extending to each side. The exterior walls are finished in a light beige or tan color, with white trim around the windows and doors. A covered front porch with supporting columns is visible at the main entrance, providing a sheltered entryway. Multiple windows are symmetrically arranged along the front and sides, and a garage is attached to one end of the building. The structure sits on a flat, paved lot, typical of suburban or residential neighborhoods.
description: A large, single-story bungalow with a cross-shaped gray shingle roof, beige walls, white trim, and a covered front porch. Symmetrical windows and an attached garage complete its suburban residential appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the covered porch, main entrance, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_1: Side/top-down view highlighting the cross-shaped roof and the depth of the central section.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back facade, additional windows, and the attached garage on the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side/top-down view emphasizing the roof structure and rear window placement.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with additional side and rear windows
